A form exist with smooth elliptical or suborbicular leaves, often as prostate shrubs specializing on coastal uplifted coral rocks up to 10m. These have been given formal taxonomic recognition as Ficus pedunculosa var. mearnsii (Merr.) Corner. This form is confined to Taiwan Islands and the northern Philippines, but might be present as small populations in coastal areas facing the Philippine Sea. 0-2000m.
